evidence.evidence_text: Freeze–thaw cycle test and damage mechanics models of alkali-activated slag concrete Yawei Fu ⇑, Liangcai Cai, Yonggen Wu College of Engineering, Air Force Engineering University, Xi’an 710038, China article info Article history: Received 12 October 2010 Received in revised form 5 December 2010 Accepted 7 December 2010 Available online 18 February 2011 Keywords: Alkali-activated slag concrete Freeze–thaw cycle Microstructure Dynamic elasticity modulus Damage mechanics models abstract Alkali-acti
Mix proportion, workability and compressive strength at 28 d of ASC are listed in Table 2, but the packet does not expose the table values; text states strengths were all above 86 MPa.

review_queue.question: Digitize or inspect the source figure/table to recover mixture-level compressive-strength values.
review_queue.suggested_action: Open the PDF page images and extract numeric strength values only if mixture labels, age, unit, and values can be confirmed.

main_risks: Compressive-strength values are not directly readable from packet text.
